_comment: 411 World Cup Sweepstake — team data. MANAGERS verified against a late-May 2026 list; they can still change before kick-off (11 June 2026), so re-check just before sending emails (Saudi Arabia especially). STAR PLAYERS are editorial picks (the recognisable talisman per team) — edit freely; the less-certain ones are Haiti, Curaçao, Cape Verde, Iraq, Panama, Tunisia, DR Congo and Australia. FUNFACT is non-football trivia. POLITICALLEANING is a broad, simplified snapshot of the governing party/leader as of mid-2026 and will shift with elections — treat as a rough guide, not gospel; for monarchies/theocracies left–right doesn't map. DEMOCRACYSTATUS follows the Economist Intelligence Unit (EIU) Democracy Index four-tier scale (full / flawed / hybrid / authoritarian). Politics is sensitive — sanity-check anything before any public use. FIXTURES/kick-off times are NOT here: pull them live from the data feed and merge by team name. Feed name mapping: South Korea='Korea Republic', Czechia='Czech Republic', Türkiye='Turkey', Ivory Coast='Côte d'Ivoire', Iran='IR Iran', USA='United States'.

teams.name: Mexico
teams.funFact: Mexico gave the world chocolate, tomatoes and chillies — staples now eaten almost everywhere.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left / left-populist (Morena, Pres. Sheinbaum)

teams.name: South Africa
teams.funFact: It's the only country with three capital cities — Pretoria, Cape Town and Bloemfontein.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left ANC leading a unity coalition with the centre-right DA (Pres. Ramaphosa)

teams.name: South Korea
teams.funFact: Home to some of the world's fastest internet and to 'kimjang', the communal making of kimchi.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left / liberal (Democratic Party, Pres. Lee Jae-myung)

teams.name: Czechia
teams.funFact: The word 'robot' was coined here, in Karel Čapek's 1920 play R.U.R.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right / populist (ANO-led coalition with the far-right SPD, PM Babiš)

teams.name: Canada
teams.funFact: It has the longest coastline of any country — over 200,000 km.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre / centre-left (Liberal Party, PM Mark Carney)

teams.name: Switzerland
teams.funFact: It has enough nuclear fallout shelters to house its entire population.
teams.politicalLeaning: Multi-party consensus government (left, centre and right share power by design)

teams.name: Qatar
teams.funFact: It has almost no natural fresh water and relies largely on desalinated seawater.
teams.politicalLeaning: Absolute monarchy (Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani) — left–right doesn't apply

teams.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
teams.funFact: Home to Perućica, one of the last primeval forests left in Europe.
teams.politicalLeaning: Ethnic power-sharing system with a rotating three-member presidency — no single governing ideology

teams.name: Brazil
teams.funFact: It holds about 60% of the Amazon rainforest and spans four time zones.
teams.politicalLeaning: Left / centre-left (Workers' Party, Pres. Lula)

teams.name: Morocco
teams.funFact: Home to the world's oldest continuously operating university, founded in 859 AD in Fez.
teams.politicalLeaning: Constitutional monarchy; elected government is centre-right (RNI), but real power rests with King Mohammed VI

teams.name: Haiti
teams.funFact: In 1804 it became the first nation founded by a successful revolt of enslaved people.
teams.politicalLeaning: No elected government — a transitional council governs amid severe instability

teams.name: Scotland
teams.funFact: Its official national animal is the unicorn.
teams.politicalLeaning: Part of the UK (Labour government, centre-left); also has a devolved SNP government (centre-left, pro-independence)

teams.name: USA
teams.funFact: It has no official language at the federal level.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right (Republican Party, Pres. Trump)

teams.name: Paraguay
teams.funFact: Its flag is the only national flag with different designs on its front and back.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right / centre-right (Colorado Party, Pres. Peña)

teams.name: Australia
teams.funFact: It's wider than the moon, and home to most of the world's most venomous snakes.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left (Labor, PM Albanese)

teams.name: Türkiye
teams.funFact: Istanbul is the only major city that sits on two continents — Europe and Asia.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right / national-conservative (AKP, Pres. Erdoğan)

teams.name: Germany
teams.funFact: It has around 20,000 castles and more than 1,500 types of beer.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right led (CDU/CSU–SPD coalition, Chancellor Merz)

teams.name: Curaçao
teams.funFact: The bright blue Curaçao liqueur is made from the dried peel of the island's laraha oranges.
teams.politicalLeaning: Self-governing parliamentary democracy within the Kingdom of the Netherlands (governing MFK, populist)

teams.name: Ivory Coast
teams.funFact: Home to the world's largest church — the Basilica of Our Lady of Peace in Yamoussoukro.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre / liberal (RHDP, Pres. Ouattara)

teams.name: Ecuador
teams.funFact: Thanks to the equatorial bulge, the summit of Mount Chimborazo is the point on Earth closest to outer space.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right / right (ADN, Pres. Noboa)

teams.name: Netherlands
teams.funFact: There are more bicycles than people, and much of the country sits below sea level.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centrist (D66-led minority coalition with the centre-right VVD and CDA, PM Jetten)

teams.name: Japan
teams.funFact: It has more than 4 million vending machines, selling everything from hot coffee to fresh eggs.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right / conservative (LDP, PM Takaichi)

teams.name: Sweden
teams.funFact: The 'right to roam' lets anyone hike and camp almost anywhere in the countryside.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right (Moderate-led, PM Kristersson, backed by the right-wing Sweden Democrats)

teams.name: Tunisia
teams.funFact: The Star Wars planet Tatooine is named after — and was filmed near — the southern town of Tataouine.
teams.politicalLeaning: Power concentrated in the presidency (Pres. Saïed) after a 2021 power grab — left–right no longer meaningful

teams.name: Belgium
teams.funFact: A Belgian, Adolphe Sax, invented the saxophone — and the country makes around 800 kinds of beer.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right / Flemish-nationalist led (N-VA, PM De Wever) in a broad coalition

teams.name: Egypt
teams.funFact: The Great Pyramid of Giza was the tallest structure built by humans for around 3,800 years.
teams.politicalLeaning: Authoritarian, military-backed (Pres. el-Sisi)

teams.name: Iran
teams.funFact: Ancient Persia gave the world the windmill and the underground 'qanat' water channels still used today.
teams.politicalLeaning: Theocratic republic — ultimate power held by the Supreme Leader, not the elected president

teams.name: New Zealand
teams.funFact: In 1893 it became the first country in the world to give women the right to vote.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right (National-led coalition, PM Luxon)

teams.name: Spain
teams.funFact: It has the world's third-highest number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left (PSOE-led coalition with the left-wing Sumar, PM Sánchez)

teams.name: Cape Verde
teams.funFact: This 10-island archipelago is the birthplace of soulful 'morna' music, made famous by Cesária Évora.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right (Movement for Democracy, PM Correia e Silva)

teams.name: Saudi Arabia
teams.funFact: It's the largest country in the world with no rivers at all.
teams.politicalLeaning: Absolute monarchy (de facto ruler Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman) — left–right doesn't apply

teams.name: Uruguay
teams.funFact: In 2013 it became the first country to fully legalise cannabis nationwide.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left / left (Broad Front, Pres. Orsi)

teams.name: France
teams.funFact: It's the most-visited country on Earth, with around 100 million tourists a year.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centrist (Pres. Macron) governing with a fragmented, hung parliament

teams.name: Senegal
teams.funFact: Lake Retba is naturally pink, coloured by salt-loving algae.
teams.politicalLeaning: Left / pan-Africanist (PASTEF, Pres. Faye & PM Sonko)

teams.name: Norway
teams.funFact: It has the world's longest road tunnel — the 24.5 km Lærdal Tunnel.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left (Labour, PM Støre)
teams.democracyStatus: Full democracy (consistently ranked the world's strongest)

teams.name: Iraq
teams.funFact: Ancient Mesopotamia, between the Tigris and Euphrates, is where writing was first invented.
teams.politicalLeaning: Sectarian power-sharing coalition (PM al-Sudani) — left–right doesn't map cleanly

teams.name: Argentina
teams.funFact: Buenos Aires has the world's widest avenue — 9 de Julio, up to 16 lanes across.
teams.politicalLeaning: Right / libertarian (La Libertad Avanza, Pres. Milei)

teams.name: Algeria
teams.funFact: It's the largest country in Africa — about 80% of it is Sahara Desert.
teams.politicalLeaning: State-dominant, military-influenced (Pres. Tebboune) — not a clear left–right system

teams.name: Austria
teams.funFact: The croissant traces back to the Viennese 'kipferl', and Salzburg was Mozart's birthplace.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right led (ÖVP–SPÖ–NEOS coalition, Chancellor Stocker)

teams.name: Jordan
teams.funFact: Home to Petra, a city carved into rose-coloured rock more than 2,000 years ago.
teams.politicalLeaning: Constitutional monarchy — King Abdullah II holds real power; the government is appointed

teams.name: Portugal
teams.funFact: Its land borders have barely changed since 1139, making it one of Europe's oldest nation-states.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right (PSD-led Democratic Alliance, PM Montenegro)

teams.name: DR Congo
teams.funFact: The Congo is the world's deepest river, plunging to over 220 metres.
teams.politicalLeaning: Dominant-presidency system (Pres. Tshisekedi), with conflict in the east

teams.name: Uzbekistan
teams.funFact: It's one of only two 'doubly landlocked' countries — you must cross two borders to reach any sea.
teams.politicalLeaning: Authoritarian, dominant-party (Pres. Mirziyoyev), gradually reforming

teams.name: Colombia
teams.funFact: It's one of the most biodiverse countries on Earth and the world's top emerald producer.
teams.politicalLeaning: Left (Historic Pact, Pres. Petro — in office until Aug 2026)

teams.name: England
teams.funFact: London opened the world's first underground railway in 1863.
teams.politicalLeaning: Part of the UK (Labour government, centre-left, PM Starmer)

teams.name: Croatia
teams.funFact: The necktie originated here — the word 'cravat' comes from 'Croat'.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right (HDZ, PM Plenković)

teams.name: Ghana
teams.funFact: In 1957 it became the first country in sub-Saharan Africa to gain independence.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-left (National Democratic Congress, Pres. Mahama)

teams.name: Panama
teams.funFact: Because of the country's curve, you can watch the sun rise over the Pacific and set over the Atlantic.
teams.politicalLeaning: Centre-right / right (Realizando Metas, Pres. Mulino)
